claim_results.claim: 20 Bias risk (lower better) 60 Sample sizes and sigma levels cited from PEAR and GCP studies; no experimental variables or methods outlined; limitations acknowledged via objections and audit; statistical significance numbers quoted but no new data; conceptual paper without direct evidence.

The quality of an observer's coupling to the Logos field is proposed to influence quantum measurement statistics. conf 0.70 > If the framework is correct, then there should be measurable differences in quantum measurement statistics depending on the quality of the observer's coupling to the Logos field.
It is a \*signature of broken coupling.\* Uncertainty: Relies on reinterpretation of established physics without direct evidence.

Radiometric dating might misinterpret pre-Fall ages because it measures post-Fall decay rates. conf 0.60 > If radioactive decay is a post-Fall quantum phenomenon ... then radiometric dating is measuring the behavior of a post-Fall substrate and projecting it backward into a pre-Fall regime where the physics was different.

The incompatibility between general relativity and quantum mechanics is framed as evidence of two distinct post- and pre-Fall states, potentially dissolving the YEC/OEC debate. conf 0.60 > The incompatibility between them is the incompatibility between the two states.
Uncertainty: Consequential claim contingent on acceptance of the entire framework.
